name: Vic Dickenson
abstract: Victor Dickenson (August 6, 1906 – November 16, 1984) was an American jazz trombonist. His career began in the 1920s and continued through musical partnerships with Count Basie (1940–41), Sidney Bechet (1941), and Earl Hines.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: we had a good little band with Eddie Hayward and Vic Dickenson... Henry Coker (PHONETIC) played trombones... I played saxaphone

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: Lester and Vic Dickenson, they used to just keep us going all the time... we were playing at a theatre in Brooklyn and the Band had just got through playing there... Vic Dickenson jumped up and commenced to playing... Basie said... they all had to commence to putting riffs behind him

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: So the Speed Webb band had Jonesey on one trumpet, Roy Eldridge on the other, Vic Dickenson on one trombone and my brother on the other trombone

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Sidney asked if I'd go in with him with a quartet, the wonderful Kenny Kersey on piano and Vic Dickenson on trombone, and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: This was Vic Dickenson, myself, Arvell Shaw, Kansas Fields, George Wein on the piano.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I noticed two trombone players that I would love to have right off the bat and that Tyree Glenn or Vic Dickenson. Those two guys are so individual. They are beautiful guys. Oh, they're lovely.
